chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195_2
text: Here, we see that the search results are structured as links whose parent element is a <h3> tag with class "r".
page: book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195_3
text: To scrape the search results, we will use a CsS selector, which was introduced in chapter 2, Scraping the Data:
page: book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195_4
text: >>> from lxml.html import fromstring >>> import requests >>> html = requests.get ('https://www.google.com/search?q=test') >>> tree = fromstring (html.content) >>> results = tree.cssselect('h3.r a') >>> results [<Element a at 0x7f3d9affeaf8>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9affe890>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9affe8e8>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9affeaa0>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9e68>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9c58>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9ec0>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9f18>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9f70>, <Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9fc8>]
page: book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195
